_meta.summary: stage0 string comparison operators >= / <= do NOT work for character range checks

symptom: ch >= "0" && ch <= "9" always evaluates false in stage0, causing all digit-detection loops to fail. JSON numeric value extraction returns -1 or 0.0 for valid input.
root_cause: stage0 C codegen emits pointer comparison for string >= / <= operators instead of lexicographic or char-code comparison. The HexaVal string type wraps a char* pointer, and >= compares pointer addresses (always arbitrary), not character values.
potentially_affected_patterns: Any .hexa file using ch >= "0" && ch <= "9" or similar char range checks
Character classification (is_alpha, is_digit, is_hex) via string comparison
workaround: Use char_code(ch, 0) for character classification. '0'=48, '9'=57, 'A'=65, 'Z'=90, 'a'=97, 'z'=122. Integer comparison works correctly in stage0.
fix_pattern: fn is_digit(ch: string) -> bool { let c = char_code(ch, 0); return c >= 48 && c <= 57 }
upstream_fix_needed: stage0 string comparison codegen (src/codegen/comparison.c or equivalent)
test_case: let ch = "0"; println(to_string(ch >= "0")); // expected: true, actual: false
